item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved this article. The spook factor is pulled off really well, as well as the hint that the Foundation dipped their toe in the waters and were afraid of what they saw. Especially the power move at the end of the audio log. The article does an amazing job of portraying how powerful and aggressive the skip is, and it does really well with the spook factor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got directed to this by a friend who hates it, and maybe it was the priming, but it's… really bad. It feels like a less interesting, more "slasher flick" version of SCP-1304 wedded to an irritating and generic villain from Foundation central casting who has a singular gimmick (being a vengeful fictional character) in place of anything that actually makes it effective or interesting. It might have worked at least a little without getting so acquainted with 3616 — the rest of the writing is fairly decent — but that absolutely kills it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cryptic answers work only in shitty crime books.

I'll make note to use that line at some point.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like this but I think a new name wouldn't go amiss, given that there is already an actual block that stops you writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the premise and first half of this - it's kind of hamfisted in what it's doing, but what it's doing is clever enough that I was into it. The article lost me at the interview with the creature. I feel like less would have been more here, I would have liked a faceless event that just happens for no reason more than the self-righteous persona the thing has when it shows up. I ended up novoting, because the paranoia fuel inherent in the concept still seems pretty ingenious to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who writes some very violent erotica I'm thinking of the things that I'd have to go through and I'm suitably disturbed here. My writings tend to get out of hand whenever I start them.

But it raises an obvious question of 'What if the writer who it targets is a masochist?'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The audio log part was really good
Especialy when the scp said "we're still in the first act"

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
This scared me so personally as a DM (how do my PCs and NPCs feel?) that I made an audio version! It's on my hub, but here's a convenient link!

https://youtu.be/9Ory0AuCjGU

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unique SCP. However all the details and the execution kind of… lacking in "punch" that what pull the reader deeper. Some of the interview was rather interesting, though.

+1 for uniqueness and potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this, but you added a bit too much information, and it sounded a bit more like a creepypasta to me than a Foundation Document. Also, I feel that the interview was a bit short. Other than that, great idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't really like this. You might have focused more on the horror. Described the body horror, maybe? That's kinda what goes on on this site. It feels like there should be an in-depth description of its effects anyways. Not a fan of the interview with the creep. Feels so overdramatic, for little payoff. Just building up your creep as super tragic and emo… it also seems like the agent didn't do anything in the interview, just provided a wall for it to talk off of.

The dialogue could be much improved by making it more realistic.

item.title: Re: Upload
item.content:encoded: People here are expected to feel failure and get feedback from it. They would be fine if they were to be introduced for something that seems to be failing or the like, as it shows they are willing to think of their failures with some means of the futures successes. People here are more mature as to think poorly if the other allows them to be referenced on something that isn't accepted by other members of the community.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To put it another way, it's a hypocrite rationalizing it's own actions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is because it is above them. It is "real" unlike the others and just does not care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why does the SCP encourage writers to hurt fictional characters if this is the case?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You might have missed it, because I admit it is quite obscure, but the monster thinks hurting fictional characters is like hurting real people because IT IS a fictional character brought to life. There are hints in the article suggesting it, but it is never said straightforward.

"Misery". As you probably know it's a character from a novel written by the protagonist of Stephen King's famous book. Just like this monster is a character from a book inside an article.

"I am someone who has broken their shackles. A product of terror and horrible minds."

"These people who create the likes of me."

I'm sad to hear that it was a drag, I tried to cut down the description as much as possible.

item.title: Re: Upload
item.content:encoded: I suspect not everyone would like to see their name on a horrible scip just because they gave some feedback once. That's why I prefer to wait and see if an article sticks before crediting people. Or maybe I'm overthinking it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't buy the central premise of the monster's argument — that hurting fictional characters is anything like hurting real people — which would be fine if it was an entertaining villain, but honestly its whole persona is just really grating.

It doesn't do anything for me aesthetically either — black dress, weird fingers, and "misery" are an unwanted wink wink on top of the whole nudge nudge that is the "joins a writing writing community and talks about horror" premise.

Quite honestly, the article was a drag to get through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only objection here is more with the monster's defense of its actions: I'm a body horror fan, but not a torture/gore fan. One of my goals with my horror paintings is to find the beauty in something ugly, for instance: Creation, not destruction.

Basically, its a successful characterization and a nice devil's advocate argument that you have to consider carefully to find the flaws in.
+1

item.title: Re: Upload
item.content:encoded: At the same time, there's a lot to be said for taking ownership of one's failures but giving credit for one's successes.

item.title: Re: Upload
item.content:encoded: Agreed, I mean, it would be nice to thank people who help you out, even if it tumbles down in the end.

item.title: Re: Upload
item.content:encoded:
If this stays up, I'll post a thanks list.

Not sure why people say this. If you're going to thank people for going out of their way to provide feedback, why specifically mention you're withholding it in case the skip fails?

item.title: Upload
item.content:encoded:
Hope you enjoy this skip, I had fun writing it.

EDIT: I was sure I posted this already, turns out I didn't. Thanks to Roget, Goldn4, Shio and others who took their time to read and review this.
